danger_signals.visual_form: Minecrawler and Shwang Shwing enemy designs
danger_signals.meaning: Extraterrestrial threat to Earth; invasion state active

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile entity appearance in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat danger; player must engage or avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Low health points indicator (Dr. Hawkins at 60 HP vs Max at 200 HP)
danger_signals.meaning: Varying susceptibility to damage; some characters in greater danger than others

danger_signals.visual_form: Kurt locked in strafe-only stance during sniper weapon deployment
danger_signals.meaning: Tactical vulnerability; player cannot retreat while aiming

safety_signals.visual_form: Parachute visual effect from Kurt's outfit
safety_signals.meaning: Safe vertical movement; enables long jumps and fall survival

safety_signals.visual_form: Max's jet pack visual attachment and thrust effects
safety_signals.meaning: Mobility and evasion capability; escape from danger zones

safety_signals.visual_form: Fuelling pumps and ammo pickups in environment

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kurt Hectic, Dr. Hawkins, Max (playable characters with distinct silhouettes)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Human-designed weapons (chain guns, sniper rifles, toasters); technological equipment; fuelling stations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Minecrawler (single remaining unit); Shwang Shwing (massive alien antagonist)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Extraterrestrial enemy designs; alien spacecraft/structures inferred from invasion context

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Invasion in progress / not yet concluded
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien enemy presence in game world (Minecrawler, Shwang Shwing)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must actively combat or evade alien entities; game flow structured around engagement with invasion forces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three distinct playable character models with unique armor/design (Kurt in Coil Suit, Max as robotic dog, Dr. Hawkins as scientist)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level design shifts based on selected character; gameplay style varies (run-and-gun for Kurt, heavy weapons for Max, puzzle-solving for Hawkins)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health point differences visible through character model proportions and equipment (Max's four arms, Dr. Hawkins' smaller frame)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tactical approach changes; Max can sustain damage longer (200 HP), Dr. Hawkins requires evasion (60 HP)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level geometry with updrafts, jumps, and fall distances
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kurt's ribbon chute and Max's jet pack used for vertical traversal; Dr. Hawkins relies on puzzle solutions for progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Each character's levels geared toward their abilities: Kurt navigates with ribbon chute through vertical spaces; Max traverses with jet pack firepower; Dr. Hawkins solves inventory puzzles
Fuelling pumps scattered for Max's jet pack recharge; ammo types reflect character specialization (unlimited ammo for Kurt's chain gun, limited for Max's specialty weapons)
Game begins 'moments after' original MDK's ending; visual presence of remaining Minecrawler and reveal of Shwang Shwing communicates that invasion threat persists despite previous apparent victory
Kurt's Coil Suit, Dr. Hawkins' dual inventories and atomic toaster, Max's four-armed robotic form visually communicate role and capabilities without explicit text

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Uses ribbon chute for vertical movement, jump extended distance, survives falls; operates chain gun for sustained fire
Weapon transforms (chain gun detaches, mounts on helmet); movement restricted to strafing only; vulnerability to flanking increases
Stands on two back legs; equips up to four guns simultaneously; can deliver high-impact firepower; highest health pool (200 HP)
Elevated mobility; can evade via fuel-based standard pack or auto-recharging atomic pack; tactical positioning advantage
Manipulates dual inventories (left/right hand); combines objects to progress; lowest health (60 HP) requires caution and evasion over direct combat
Present as obstacles and combat encounters; communicate invasion continuation; trigger character-appropriate defensive responses

notes: MDK2 primarily communicates world-state through character-specific mechanics and level design rather than environmental visual polish (noted as positive reception for graphics but T2 tier limits specific visual descriptors). The game's narrative tension—false victory in original game followed by alien revelation—manifests through gameplay structure: initial belief of safety replaced by persistent threat. Faction identity is minimal; emphasis is on player capability variation and alien opposition. Behavioral tells are mechanical (health pools, weapon mounting) rather than AI-driven animation states. The inventory system for Dr. Hawkins uniquely communicates puzzle-progression world-state through object combination logic rather than visual environmental storytelling.
